lines.text: As a member of the works council at NorthStar Logistics AG, I want to give my view on the new AI tools.
Some colleagues are really worried about the plan.
They fear that the software will take over their tasks, for example the scanning of parcels at gate two, which two workers do all day.
Others feel watched, because the tools record how fast we work, like the counter that already shows our packing speed on the screen.
A third group simply finds the new programs hard to learn.
Still, there are good reasons for the tools, so I want to look at the other side.
They can handle routine work, for example filling in the same delivery form again and again, so we get more time for our customers.
They can also catch errors in the delivery lists, such as a wrong postcode, before a parcel leaves the depot.
In the end, I think we should agree to the tools if the company trains everyone and sets fair rules.
